title: DataScouteR: Platform Components
text:
DataScouteR is divided into five specialized modules, each designed to solve a specific problem in the scouting workflow.


The landing module for the application.

Team Summary: Displays the Current Ability (CA) average for the entire squad and the total player count.
Interactive Squad List: A searchable table of all players in the selected team. Selecting a player here “primes” the rest of the application for deep analysis.




A dual-visual approach to individual performance.

Attribute Profile: A bar chart showing raw 1-20 ratings across position-specific metrics (e.g., ShotStopping for GKs or Creativity for FWs).
Global Distribution: A density plot showing where the player sits compared to every other player of that position in the database.
Percentile Ranking: A textual output identifying the player’s “Global Rank” (e.g., “Better than 85% of Midfielders”).




A powerful multi-criteria filter for identifying new talent.

Position-Specific Logic: The filters change dynamically based on the target position.
Threshold Filtering: Set minimum ratings for multiple attributes simultaneously.
CSV Export: Download your custom shortlists for external reporting.




A comparative tool for recruitment gap analysis. * Dual Selectors: Compare an “Internal Player” from your squad against an “External Target.” * Lollipop Chart: A visual gap analysis tool that highlights exactly where a target is better (or worse) than your current starter.



The most interactive component for squad building.

Reactive Roster: A “Sandbox” version of your team.
Transfer Market: Search for players globally and “Add” them to your squad.
Projected CA: A real-time calculation of your team’s average quality. If you remove a low-CA player and add a high-CA player, the dashboard updates instantly.

title: DataScouteR: Platform Components
text:
Technical Details

Scale: All metrics are normalized on a 1-20 scale.
Database: Aggregated data from global divisions including GK, DF, MF, and FW groups.
Language: Built entirely in R using the Shiny framework, bslib for modern UI components and the DataScouteR package.
